details.text: Derivative: خَانَتَا – “They both betrayed” from the verb خَانَ – يَخُونُ – خِيَانَةً which means to betray, use deception and go behind someone’s back.

Derivative: الْقَانِتِينَ “Those who are devoutly obedient or subservient i.e., to Allah” from قَنَتَ – يَقْنُتُ – قُنُوت (Qunoot) which means to be obedient, submissive and to have humility. It is used to describe how the wife should be with her husband but here it refers to the original subservience and obedience to Allah and humility before Him. Even the Qunoot to the husband is only done in Qunoot (subservience and obedience) to Allah.
Derivative: صَغَتْ from صَغَا – يَصْغُو – صَغْوًا means to lean or incline towards something.
Derivative: تَظَاهَرَا from تَظَاهَرَ – يَتَظاهَرُ – تَظَاهُرًا means to work together or assist each other against someone, to gang up together or collaborate.

Derivative: سَائِحَاتٍ means to give up worldly things in the case where سَاحَ (Saaha) means to give up worldly things and is a form of Zuhd (زُهْد). It also means to fast, to leave worldly appetites.

Derivative: غِلَاظٌ means to be harsh, stern and stubborn. Refers to the Angels presiding over the fire. It means that they are not swayed away by anything from overseeing the inflicting of punishment.

Derivative: تُوبُوا – “Turn back in repentance”. This is the plural command form from تَابَ – يَتُوبُ. It has three Masdars: تَوْبَةً (Tawbah), تَوْبًا (Tawb) and مَتَابًا (Mataab). مَتَابًا (Mataab) is the strongest of the three. تَوْبًا (Tawb) is general and refers to all kinds of repentance. تَوْبَةً (Tawbah) has the Masdar form which is known as مَصْدَر مَرَّة (The Masdar of a single occurrence) like ضَرْبَةً (Darbah) which means a single strike. This implies that this Tawbah should be one which is radical and complete.
Derivative: نَصُوحًا – Means sincere as the verb نَصَحَ includes the meanings of إِخْلاص (sincerity).
